_comment: Shared, version-controlled gh-tool-guard policy. defaultMode='allow' — gh is mostly safe (read-mostly + benign mutations like issue create / pr comment). Deny only the few credential-clearing and resource-deleting verbs; warn on PR-body autoclose keywords (which silently auto-close issues at merge time) and on claude-only sensitive mutations. Patterns are tightened to avoid false positives on hypothetical 'verb-suffix' commands. See scripts/tool-guard/gh/POLICY.md for design notes.

deny.message: auth logout would clear gh's credential cache. If you really mean it, run /usr/bin/gh auth logout directly. Otherwise: this likely fired by accident — check your command.
auth logout (with flags) would clear gh's credential cache. If you really mean it, run /usr/bin/gh auth logout directly.

Deleting a GitHub repository is permanent and cascades to all forks, issues, PRs, and Actions. Almost never the right command from CLI — do it from the web UI where the action requires typing the repo name.
Deleting a GitHub repository is permanent and cascades to all forks, issues, PRs, and Actions. Almost never the right command from CLI.
Removing an SSH key revokes that key's git access. Coordinate with the key owner before removing.
Removing an SSH key revokes that key's git access. Coordinate first.
Removing a GPG key invalidates signature verification on all commits signed with it.
Removing a GPG key invalidates signature verification on all commits signed with it.
Deleting a repository / org / environment secret can break running workflows that depend on it.
Deleting a repository / org / environment secret can break running workflows that depend on it. Coordinate first.
Deleting an Actions variable can break running workflows.
Deleting an Actions variable can break running workflows. Coordinate first.
Deleting a release removes the asset uploads + the tag annotation. Users who pinned lose access.
Deleting a release removes the asset uploads + the tag annotation. Users who pinned lose access.

warn.message: pr merge under Claude — make sure the PR has been reviewed by a human and CI is green. Use --auto if you want GitHub to wait for required checks.
pr close (without merging) abandons the work. Coordinate with the PR author.
Closing an issue under Claude — make sure the work is actually done (or that this is intentional triage).
Creating a release publishes a tag + asset bundle visible to all users. Make sure the version, notes, and target are correct.

PR body contains a GitHub auto-close keyword (fix/fixes/fixed/close/closes/closed/resolve/resolves/resolved) followed by #NNN. GitHub will auto-close the linked issue at merge time, regardless of whether the issue's DoD is met. Use 'Part of #NNN' or 'Addresses #NNN' instead if you don't want that side effect. Word-boundary aware (won't false-positive on 'prefixes #'). Scoped to 'pr create' and 'pr edit' (issue bodies / pr comments are exempt). Covers all three arg forms: --body value, --body=value, -b value.
